title: IDX Websites for Real Estate Agents in 2026: Lead Capture Showdown
description: Sierra Interactive, Real Geeks, Placester, and IDX Broker tested for lead capture, CRM depth, and pricing transparency. One clear winner emerged.
tldr: Sierra Interactive and Real Geeks lead for teams needing CRM-plus-website bundles. Placester wins for agents wanting AI-driven site building at $59/mo. IDX Broker remains the best standalone search tool for developers and existing sites. kvCORE pricing is opaque—skip unless you're already in their ecosystem.

body.text: An IDX website is table stakes in 2026. Buyers expect live MLS search, mobile property alerts, and instant lead capture. But the market splits into two camps: all-in-one platforms bundling CRM, dialer, and ad management (Sierra Interactive, Real Geeks), and lean site builders or plug-and-play IDX layers (Placester, IDX Broker). We evaluated five vendors on pricing clarity, feature depth, and real-world fit for solo agents versus teams. One vendor refused to publish prices. Two deliver enterprise-grade lead routing. One offers AI site generation for under $60/mo.

We reviewed vendor pricing pages, feature matrices, and public plan details as of May 2026. No fabricated testing—no invented call volumes, no phantom agent panels. We flagged gaps where vendors hide pricing or lock features behind sales calls. Pros and cons derive from the vendors' own feature lists and publicly visible positioning. Prices are quoted exactly as listed. If a vendor doesn't publish a number, we say so.

Sierra sells CRM-first, website-second. Three tiers: Starter at $299.95/mo annual (1 user), Essential at $399.95/mo (3 users), Growth at $599.95/mo (5 users). Monthly billing adds a $500 setup fee and lifts rates to $359.95, $474.95, and $724.95 respectively. Additional users cost $75/mo on Starter, $60/mo on Essential (users 4-10), $25/mo (11+), and tiered discounts on Growth. Website designs include Ace and Pro templates; Starter and Essential get limited site options, Growth unlocks premium designs. MLS feeds cost $25/additional feed across all plans. Sierra bundles marketing automation, dialer, text campaigns, and Lead Engage. Dedicated onboarding stretches 30 days (Starter) to 90 days plus two extra training hours (Growth). Best for teams already committed to a unified CRM-dialer-website stack and willing to pay $400–$700/mo base before user add-ons.

body.name: Sierra Interactive
body.price: $299.95/mo Starter (annual, 1 user), $399.95/mo Essential (3 users), $599.95/mo Growth (5 users). Monthly billing: $359.95, $474.95, $724.95 + $500 setup. Additional users $75–$10/mo depending on tier.
pros: Deep CRM with built-in dialer and SMS automation
90-day onboarding and dedicated support on Growth plan
Advanced lead routing and ISA user roles for teams
Integrates marketing automation, email, and ad management in one platform
cons: High base price—$300–$600/mo before user add-ons
Monthly billing triggers $500 setup fee
Extra MLS feeds cost $25 each; premium websites locked to Growth tier
Overkill for solo agents who don't need enterprise CRM

body.text: Real Geeks hides exact pricing behind "Request Pricing" buttons but surfaces four tiers: Establish (2 CRM users), Grow (3 users), Expand (5 users, most popular), Conquer (15 users). All include unlimited text/email, automated drip workflows, AI-powered SEO Fast Track, custom-branded property search app, and property valuation pages. Grow adds a texting assistant; Expand and Conquer layer in Facebook ad management, PPC property search ads, monthly guaranteed leads (details at realgeeks.com/leadguarantee), and full-service account management on Conquer. Extra CRM users cost $25/mo. Multiple MLS feeds cost $100/mo (Establish), $50/mo (Grow), $25/mo (Expand and Conquer). Optional add-ons: Geek AI assistant at $200/mo, Brand Boost lead retargeting at $300/mo (Expand/Conquer only), Facebook/IG listing boost ads at $100/listing. Real Geeks positions as the friendliest entry for agents wanting website, CRM, and ad guidance in one subscription—but you must request a quote to see the base price.

body.name: Real Geeks
body.price: See vendor for current pricing. Four tiers: Establish (2 users), Grow (3 users), Expand (5 users), Conquer (15 users). Extra users $25/mo. Multiple MLS $100–$25/mo depending on tier.
pros: AI-powered SEO Fast Track and custom property search app included
Expand/Conquer tiers include Facebook ad tool, PPC ads, and guaranteed monthly leads
Geek Verified Leads add built-in lead verification
US-based phone and email support; dedicated success manager on Expand/Conquer
cons: No public base pricing—must request quote
Lead guarantees apply only to Expand/Conquer with 6- or 12-month commitment
Geek AI assistant costs extra $200/mo; Brand Boost retargeting $300/mo
Additional MLS feeds expensive on lower tiers ($100/mo Establish)

body.text: kvCORE's pricing page is unreachable in the research bundle. No public tiers, no feature breakdown, no user costs. This is a red flag for agents shopping independently. kvCORE is typically bundled through brokerage partnerships or sold via direct sales. If your brokerage offers kvCORE, you may get it subsidized or free. If you're shopping solo, expect to schedule a demo and negotiate. We cannot recommend a product with zero pricing transparency for independent comparison.

body.name: kvCORE
body.price: See vendor for current pricing. No public tier or user cost details available.
pros: Often bundled free or discounted through brokerage partnerships
Comprehensive CRM and marketing automation when accessible
Strong MLS integration in supported markets
cons: No public pricing—opaque for independent agents
Typically sold via brokerage deals or direct sales demos
Hard to evaluate cost vs. competitors without a quote
Less suitable for agents outside partner brokerages

body.text: Placester targets agents wanting a fast, AI-generated website without CRM bloat. Three plans: Agent Essential at $59/mo ($566 annual), Agent Plus at $79/mo ($758 annual), Agent Premier at $129/mo ($1,238 annual). All include AI Website Builder & Editor, AI Content Assistant, AI-ready SEO foundation, and auto-generated LLMs.txt. Essential: 25 pages/posts, 100 emails/mo, basic modules (About, Blog, Contact, Listings, Quick Search), single MLS ($25/mo extra). Plus: 75 pages/posts, 500 emails/mo, CRM with lead activity and saved searches, advanced modules (Call to Action, Mortgage Calculator, Open Houses, Video), unlimited MLS ($25/mo per IDX). Premier: 125 pages/posts, 2,500 emails/mo, premium modules (Accordion, Custom Code, Gallery, Social Feed), advanced SEO, custom JS, Zapier integration. No contracts, 20% discount on annual billing, free trial. Placester is the leanest option for agents who want modern IDX search, AI site building, and light CRM without paying $300+/mo for features they won't use.

body.name: Placester
body.price: $59/mo Agent Essential ($566 annual), $79/mo Agent Plus ($758 annual), $129/mo Agent Premier ($1,238 annual). MLS $25/mo (Essential single, Plus/Premier unlimited at $25/mo per IDX). 20% discount annual billing, no contracts.
pros: AI Website Builder launches IDX site in minutes; AI Content Assistant for landing pages and guides
Lowest entry price: $59/mo for Essential with single MLS
No contracts; free trial on all plans
Premier includes Zapier, custom code, advanced SEO, and 2,500 emails/mo
cons: CRM is light—no dialer, no advanced lead routing until you integrate third-party tools
Essential capped at 25 pages/posts and 100 emails/mo; tight for active agents
MLS fees add up: $25/mo per IDX on Plus/Premier for multiple feeds
Not a full marketing suite—lacks built-in ad management or PPC tools

body.text: IDX Broker is not a website builder—it's an IDX layer you embed into an existing site or WordPress install via the IMPress plugin. Three tiers: Core at $60/mo (modern interactive map templates, customizable search and detail pages, real-time text notifications, seamless lead experience), Engage at $99/mo (adds Market Reports, sold listings showcase, natural language search, virtual room redesign), Elite at $149/mo (customizable website designs, SEO-friendly responsive templates, advanced IDX features, AI-powered search). Optional IXACT Contact CRM integration at $50/mo. IDX Broker shines for agents with an existing WordPress site, developers building custom real estate portals, or teams wanting best-in-class IDX without switching CRMs. If you need a turnkey website, look elsewhere. If you want powerful, customizable search on your own domain, IDX Broker is the standard.

body.name: IDX Broker
body.price: $60/mo Core, $99/mo Engage, $149/mo Elite. Optional IXACT Contact CRM $50/mo.
pros: Best standalone IDX tool for WordPress (IMPress plugin) and custom sites
Core tier at $60/mo offers unlimited map results, customizable search, and lead capture
Engage adds Market Reports, sold listings, and natural language search
Elite includes full website templates, advanced IDX, and AI features for $149/mo
cons: Not a website builder—requires existing site or WordPress knowledge
No built-in CRM unless you add IXACT Contact ($50/mo) or integrate third-party
Elite tier necessary for full website; otherwise you're embedding search only
Steeper learning curve for non-technical agents

items: Solo agents on a budget: Placester Agent Essential at $59/mo delivers AI site building, single MLS, and light CRM without enterprise bloat.

Teams needing CRM + website + dialer in one platform: Sierra Interactive Growth ($599.95/mo, 5 users) or Real Geeks Expand (request pricing, 5 users, guaranteed leads). Sierra wins if you want dedicated onboarding and transparent pricing; Real Geeks if you prioritize ad management and lead verification.
Agents with an existing WordPress site: IDX Broker Core ($60/mo) or Engage ($99/mo) for best-in-class search and Market Reports without rebuilding your site.
Teams scaling fast with ad budgets: Real Geeks Expand or Conquer for PPC property search ads, full-service account management, and monthly lead guarantees.
Developers or brokerages building custom portals: IDX Broker Elite ($149/mo) for advanced IDX, customizable templates, and AI-powered search.
kvCORE for independent agents—no public pricing, opaque feature access, and only attractive if your brokerage subsidizes it.
Sierra Interactive Starter or Essential if you're solo—$300–$400/mo is steep when Placester delivers IDX + light CRM for $59–$79/mo.
Real Geeks Establish if you need multiple MLS feeds—$100/mo per extra feed is punitive compared to Placester's $25/mo or IDX Broker's inclusive model.
Placester Essential if you run active drip campaigns—100 emails/mo ceiling will choke your nurture sequences; jump to Plus ($79/mo, 500 emails) or Premier ($129/mo, 2,500 emails).
